events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: plastic shrinkage cracks in concrete, in-service. Diagnose cause. Plastic shrinkage cracks occur when surface moisture evaporates faster than bleed water rises, due to rapid drying conditions (high temp, low humidity, wind) and inadequate curing/finishing practices. ACI 302 and 308 cover this. The fault is inadequate curing / failure to protect surface from rapid moisture loss during finishing/curing. Since in-service and cracks present, component is degraded. Decision fail because non-compliant with curing requirements. Risk medium.
